ontology.foodon_gap_note: No FoodOn term exists specifically for Korean yangnyeom (sweet-spicy glazed) boneless fried chicken. Closest defensible match is the generic fried chicken food product term. Gap noted.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers cooked fried chicken broadly. No specific code exists for Korean yangnyeom-glazed preparation. Sauce coating (gochujang, rice syrup, soy sauce) is not captured in this code.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for Korean yangnyeom fried chicken. Not clinically coded as a distinct food entity.

serving.description: 1 portion of boneless fried chicken (~400 g)
serving.natural_language_portion: A standard single-person serving of boneless Yang Nyum chicken is approximately 400 g, equivalent to roughly 8–10 boneless pieces coated in sweet-spicy sauce, as commonly portioned at Korean fried chicken restaurants in Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, fried (SR Legacy ID 05062) adapted with sauce macro contribution estimated from USDA Branded and Korean culinary composition references
nutrient_source.organization: U.S. Department of Agriculture, Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No exact entry for Korean yangnyeom boneless fried chicken exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Base fried chicken values (protein, fat, energy) are anchored to USDA SR Legacy entry 05062 (chicken, broilers or fryers, meat only, fried). Sauce contribution (sugar, sodium, carbohydrate uplift from gochujang, rice syrup, soy sauce) is estimated using USDA FoodData Central branded gochujang and soy sauce entries. The input record's per_100g values (energy 300 kcal, protein 16.67 g, fat 18.89 g, carbohydrate 15.56 g, sodium 555.56 mg) are broadly consistent with this adapted composite and are retained with medium confidence. Micronutrients (calcium 11.11 mg, iron 0.89 mg, cholesterol 66.67 mg, vitamin C 1.11 mg) are plausible for fried chicken with sauce and are retained. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, potassium_mg, and remaining micronutrients not reported in the input are set to -1 as detailed fatty acid and full micronutrient profiles for this composite dish are not available in a single approved source.

gi_source.title: Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC. International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008. Diabetes Care. 2008;31(12):2281-2283. — Analogous estimate: battered/crumbed fried chicken with sweet sauce coating approximated from mixed fried chicken and sweet sauce GI data.
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care (peer-reviewed journal)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Korean yangnyeom boneless fried chicken. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on: (1) battered fried chicken GI ~60–65 from University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for crumbed/battered fried chicken; (2) upward adjustment for the high-GI rice syrup and sugar glaze in the yangnyeom sauce, which raises the effective GI of the composite dish. Glycemic load per 400 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (65 × 62.24) / 100 ≈ 40.5.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 555.56 mg/100 g — exceeds the 400 mg/100 g threshold; driven by soy sauce, gochujang, and added salt in the yangnyeom sauce.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid or strictly limit boneless Yang Nyum chicken due to its very high sodium content (556 mg/100 g), which significantly contributes to daily sodium load and blood pressure elevation; if consumed, request reduced-sauce preparation and limit portion to no more than 100–150 g.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Sugar 7.78 g/100 g with dietary fibre only 0.67 g/100 g — triggers the high-sugar, low-fibre gate; combined with a medium-to-high GI (estimated 65) and 15.56 g/100 g total carbohydrate from wheat flour batter and rice syrup glaze.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid boneless Yang Nyum chicken due to its high sugar content (7.78 g/100 g), low fibre (0.67 g/100 g), and medium-high glycaemic index (~65), which together produce a high glycaemic load per serving (~40 GL units for 400 g) that is likely to cause significant postprandial blood glucose elevation.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation with 18.89 g/100 g total fat, 4.44 g/100 g saturated fat, 0.11 g/100 g trans fat, and 555.56 mg/100 g sodium — all unfavourable for cardiovascular risk.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid boneless Yang Nyum chicken due to its combination of high total fat (18.89 g/100 g), saturated fat (4.44 g/100 g), detectable trans fat (0.11 g/100 g), and very high sodium (556 mg/100 g), all of which adversely affect lipid profiles and blood pressure; occasional consumption should be limited to small portions with sauce on the side.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that directly worsen joint inflammation; high sugar (7.78 g/100 g) further promotes pro-inflammatory mediators.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried Yang Nyum chicken as the deep-frying process generates AGEs that exacerbate joint inflammation; a grilled or baked preparation with reduced sugar sauce would substantially lower the inflammatory burden.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Boneless Yang Nyum Chicken is a Korean-style deep-fried chicken dish consisting of boneless chicken pieces coated in a crispy wheat flour batter, deep-fried until golden, and then generously glazed with yangnyeom sauce — a sticky, sweet-spicy Korean condiment made from gochujang (fermented Korean chilli paste), soy sauce, rice syrup, garlic, ginger, sesame oil, and sugar. The dish is popular in Korean fried chicken restaurants and is widely available in Singapore through delivery platforms and Korean casual dining outlets. The texture is crispy on the outside with a tender, juicy interior, and the sauce delivers a bold combination of sweetness, heat, and umami. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides approximately 300 kcal, 16.67 g protein, 18.89 g fat (4.44 g saturated, 0.11 g trans), 15.56 g carbohydrate, 7.78 g sugar, 0.67 g dietary fibre, and 555.56 mg sodium. The high sodium content (driven by soy sauce and gochujang) and high sugar content (from rice syrup and added sugar in the sauce) make this dish clinically unsuitable for individuals managing hypertension, Type 2 Diabetes, or cardiovascular disease. The deep-frying method also generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), which are pro-inflammatory and of concern for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. A standard serving is approximately 400 g (8–10 boneless pieces), delivering roughly 1200 kcal, 62 g carbohydrate, and 2222 mg sodium per serving — well above recommended daily sodium limits for most clinical populations.

confidence_reason: No exact entry for Korean yangnyeom boneless fried chicken exists in HPB Singapore Foodo or USDA FoodData Central. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy fried chicken (entry 05062) with sauce macro contribution estimated from USDA branded gochujang and soy sauce entries. The input record's per_100g values are broadly consistent with this adapted composite and are retained. GI is an analogous estimate. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g are set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for this composite Korean fried chicken dish with yangnyeom sauce is not available in USDA FoodData Central or HPB Singapore Foodo as of 2026-04-16. 2. potassium_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: full micronutrient and moisture data for this composite dish are not available in a single approved source. 3. trans_fat_g is set to 0.11 g/100 g as reported in the input record; this is plausible for deep-fried commercial chicken products. 4. Nutrient values are adapted from USDA SR Legacy fried chicken (ID 05062) with sauce contribution estimated from USDA FoodData Central branded gochujang and soy sauce entries; the input record values are broadly consistent and retained with medium confidence. 5. GI of 65 is an analogous estimate based on battered fried chicken GI data from the University of Sydney GI Database and adjusted upward for the high-GI rice syrup and sugar glaze. 6. Glycemic load per 400 g serving = (65 × 62.24) / 100 ≈ 40.5; the glycemic_load_per_serving field is set to 40.5 for the 400 g default serving. 7. Halal status is not confirmed; gochujang and soy sauce may contain non-halal fermentation agents depending on brand. 8. The input record listed two portions both marked is_default: true; the 400 g portion is used as the primary serving size for GL calculation as it represents a single-person portion. 9. No zero-macro justification is required as all core macros (protein_g 16.67, fat_g 18.89, carbohydrate_g 15.56) are non-zero. 10. This dish is served in Singapore and is of Korean cuisine origin; multilingual names are provided for Chinese (Simplified and Traditional), Malay, Tamil, Hindi, and Korean as required by G6 for a Korean dish served in the Singaporean context. Chinese simplified: 无骨辣酱炸鸡; Chinese traditional: 無骨辣醬炸雞; Malay: Ayam Goreng Pedas Manis Korea Tanpa Tulang; Tamil: எலும்பில்லா யாங் நியும் கோழி; Hindi: बोनलेस यांग न्यूम चिकन; Korean: 양념 치킨 (뼈 없는).

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 2: Corrected ontology match types from 'approximate' to 'closest_parent'.

Iteration 3: Added mandatory local_names for chinese_simplified ('无骨辣酱炸鸡'), chinese_traditional ('無骨辣醬炸雞'), malay ('Ayam Goreng Pedas Manis Korea Tanpa Tulang'), tamil ('எலும்பில்லா யாங் நியும் கோழி'), and hindi ('बोनलेस यांग न्यूम चिकन') to satisfy G6 multilingual naming compliance for Korean dish served in Singapore context.

Iteration 3: Added all five new local_names strings verbatim to flat aliases array and to aliases_by_language.chinese, aliases_by_language.malay, aliases_by_language.tamil, and aliases_by_language.hindi to satisfy Q8 aliases consistency rule.
